long-title: Children and young people leaving school with low health literacy

sections.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: Education system that is not working properly
Teaching curriculum that is too full
Carers that no-one knows about e.g. relatives
lack of teaching about health and illness
Children and young people not feeling part of society
Health literacy in social work training (see also problem)

sections.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Engaging with senior managers in schools
Mapping out children and young peoples' teaching organisations

sections.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: *Setting up children and young peoples' reading groups
*Training courses online and / or face to face

sections.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: Children and young people having better health and well-being
Children and young people leading healthier lives
Systems to map and monitor action plans

sections.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: Reading counting and speaking skills for health will be better
More staff will have been trained
More action plans will be in place in places where children and young people learn

sections.long-title: What short term changes will we see?
content: Mapping out of institutions and places to be trained

sections.long-title: What do we think will happen in the longer term (3 years)? For example, increased health literacy, better disease control, improved health
content: Improved health literacy in children and young people
Healthier lifestyle - exercise, diet, smoking, alcohol

sections.long-title: What are the current activities in this area?
